why: Modern, composable CLI definition from the unjs ecosystem with automatic ESM optimization and zero-dependency help generation.
alternativeRejected: oclif (excessive boilerplate and heavy dependency tree for a lightweight audit tool)
aiCompat: High; utilizes clear, declarative object structures that are easy for agents to extend.
gotcha: Smaller ecosystem than commander, but more idiomatic for modern TypeScript monorepos.

why: The official standard for building MCP servers to ensure native compatibility with LLM agents.
alternativeRejected: Custom JSON-RPC (lacks community middleware and standard tool-call schema definitions)
aiCompat: Excellent; industry-standard patterns are well-represented in current training data.
gotcha: None; it is the mandatory protocol for the agent-first goal.

why: Fastest installation and intelligent task caching for the core/cli/mcp package split.
alternativeRejected: Nx (too architecturally opinionated and complex for a utility CLI)
aiCompat: High; simple turbo.json configuration is very resilient to agent modification.
gotcha: Turborepo remote caching setup can be nuanced in CI environments.

why: Zero-config esbuild-based bundler that handles dual CJS/ESM and automatically generates d.ts files.
alternativeRejected: tsc (lacks bundling and minification required for single-binary distribution)
aiCompat: Excellent; uses a single simple config or CLI flags that agents handle reliably.

why: Blazing fast unit and integration testing with native ESM support and Jest-compatible API.
alternativeRejected: Jest (significant overhead and configuration complexity in TypeScript/ESM environments)
aiCompat: Perfect; leverages existing Jest training data while providing better ESM reliability.
gotcha: Browser-mode testing requires separate configuration for Playwright integration.

why: Consolidates linting and formatting into a single Rust-powered tool that is 100x faster than the ESLint/Prettier stack.
alternativeRejected: ESLint + Prettier (slow startup times and frequent plugin version conflicts)
aiCompat: Moderate; follows strict, logical defaults that prevent the style-drift often caused by AI-generated code.
gotcha: Does not support some niche ESLint plugins; requires manual verification for custom a11y rules.

why: TypeScript-first schema declaration that provides both runtime validation and static type safety for project findings.
alternativeRejected: Valibot (smaller bundle but lacks the rich ecosystem of integrations needed for CLI tools)
aiCompat: Industry standard; agents write and maintain Zod schemas with near-zero hallucination.

why: Industry standard for human-editable configurations and Markdown-based knowledge base frontmatter.
alternativeRejected: JSON (hostile for human comments and long-form guidance documentation)
aiCompat: Excellent; YAML is a native format for LLM reasoning and configuration tasks.

why: Programmatic library invocation allows for deterministic measurement and fine-grained control within the MCP context.
alternativeRejected: CLI invocation (parsing shell output is fragile and adds process management overhead)
aiCompat: High; clear programmatic APIs are well-documented and predictable for agents.
gotcha: Lighthouse adds a significant dependency footprint; must be lazy-loaded.

why: Fastest combination for static code introspection and lightweight DOM simulation for non-browser audits.
alternativeRejected: jsdom (too slow for high-volume static component analysis)
aiCompat: High; follows standard compiler and DOM APIs that agents already use for scraping/auditing.
gotcha: happy-dom lacks full CSS layout engine support for high-fidelity visual 'judged' findings.

why: Leverages existing ecosystem tools for model reconciliation and verdict consensus as defined in the Scaffold methodology.
alternativeRejected: Direct SDKs (requires complex manual implementation of retry logic and multi-provider auth)
aiCompat: Excellent; natively integrated into the agent's existing workflow skills.
gotcha: Adds a runtime dependency on the presence of the mmr CLI.

why: Modular, community-vetted engine for calculating Flesch-Kincaid and Gunning Fog scores on interface copy.
alternativeRejected: automated-readability (less extensible and harder to integrate into a unified analysis pipeline)
aiCompat: High; straightforward API with clear input/output expectations.

why: Provides a robust local persistence layer for stable finding identity, waivers, and re-audit history.
alternativeRejected: Flat JSON files (high risk of race conditions and slow for matching findings across thousands of lines of state)
aiCompat: High; SQL and Drizzle schemas are very intuitive for AI agents to query and migrate.
gotcha: Requires native bindings which can occasionally complicate Homebrew/npx distribution if not pre-compiled.
